turns.content: What brings you in today?

Hi, Doctor. I just got back from a trip three days ago, and for the past five days I've had persistent diarrhea—like, really frequent, watery stools. On top of that, I can't keep even sips of water down without vomiting. I feel incredibly weak and dehydrated, and I'm starting to get worried because this isn't letting up.
When did this start?

It started about five days ago, actually while I was still on my trip. I began having loose stools and felt nauseous, and then the vomiting kicked in a day later. I've been dealing with it ever since.
Can you describe the pain or discomfort? Where exactly do you feel it?

The pain is mostly in my lower abdomen—cramping, off and on, and it gets worse right before I need to run to the bathroom. It's not sharp or stabbing, more like a dull, squeezing ache that comes in waves. Sometimes it's accompanied by nausea, which makes me feel even more miserable.
Have you had any fever or chills?

Yes, I have. I checked my temperature at home and it was 38.3°C. I've also had some chills and felt hot and cold off and on, especially at night. It's definitely been making me feel achy all over, too.
What medications are you currently taking?

I'm not currently taking any prescription medications. The only thing I've tried is some over-the-counter anti-diarrheal medicine, but I couldn't keep it down because of the vomiting. Also, I want you to know I have a penicillin allergy—just in case you're thinking of prescribing anything.
Do you have any chronic medical conditions?

No, I don't have any chronic medical conditions. I'm generally healthy—no diabetes, high blood pressure, or anything like that. I do fly frequently for work, but aside from that, I don't have any ongoing health issues.
Are you allergic to any medications?
Yes, I'm allergic to penicillin. I found out a few years ago when I had a bad reaction—hives and swelling—so I avoid it completely. Please keep that in mind if we need to use antibiotics.
Have you tried anything to relieve the symptoms?

Yes, I tried an over-the-counter anti-diarrheal once, but I vomited it up within an hour. I've also been trying to sip plain water and clear broth, but it's been tough keeping anything down. Honestly, nothing has provided any real relief, which is why I'm here.
Does anything make it better or worse?

Honestly, nothing really makes it better—I've been avoiding solid food entirely, but even clear liquids come back up sometimes. The only thing that helps a little is lying still, but as soon as I move around or try to get up, the cramping and urgency get worse.
How has this been affecting your daily life?

It's been completely debilitating, to be honest. As a flight attendant, I'm on my feet for hours, but I can barely get off the couch right now. I feel dizzy and weak, and rushing to the bathroom every hour or so makes it impossible to function, let alone work. I'm supposed to have another trip in two days, but I can't imagine being able to manage that.
Do you smoke or drink alcohol?

I don't smoke at all—never have. I do drink alcohol occasionally, usually a glass of wine with dinner or after a long flight, but I haven't had anything since this started because it just makes me feel worse.
Has anyone in your family had similar problems?

No, not that I know of. No one in my household or immediate family has had diarrhea or vomiting recently. It's just me. I was on an international flight before the symptoms started, so I'm wondering if I picked something up during the trip.
Have you experienced anything like this before?

I've had a mild bout of traveler's diarrhea a couple of times before, but nothing like this. That usually cleared up in a day or two with rest and fluids. This time, it's been relentless for five days, and I've never had vomiting with it before. It's definitely different—and much more intense.
On a scale of 1 to 10, how would you rate what you're going through right now — physically, and also emotionally?

Physically, I'd say I'm around a 7 or 8 right now—the cramping is pretty persistent and the weakness is really getting to me. Emotionally, maybe a 6—I'm frustrated and irritable because this is interfering with my job and I can't seem to get a break from it, plus I'm worried I might be dehydrated.
Is there anything else you think I should know?

One thing I should mention—I was in Southeast Asia for a layover right before this started, specifically a couple of days in Bangkok. I ate street food there, so I've been wondering if that could be the source. I didn't have
And how have you been coping with all of this?

Honestly, I've just been trying to rest as much as I can and force myself to sip fluids even when I don't want to. But I'm getting frustrated because nothing seems to be helping—I'm still weak, still nauseous, and still running to the bathroom constantly. I'm usually pretty self-sufficient, so feeling this helpless is wearing on me. I really need something that actually works, because I can't keep going like this.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

I understand you're asking again, but I've been trying to rest and sip fluids as best I can, even though it feels like a losing battle. I keep a glass of water next to me, but I vomit after a few sips a lot of the time. It's just not getting better, and I'm starting to feel pretty worn down and anxious that I'm not recovering on my own.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

I'm sorry, but I already answered that. I'm coping by resting and trying to stay hydrated, but it's not working and I'm getting more frustrated each day. Is there something you're specifically concerned about that you need me to repeat?
And how have you been coping with all of this?

With respect, I've already told you—I'm resting and trying to drink fluids, but it's not helping and I'm getting more irritable by the day. I feel like I'm just spinning my wheels here, and I really need some actual medical help, not just repeated questions about my coping.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

I understand you're trying to get a full picture, but I've answered that three times now. I'm not coping well—I'm stuck on my couch, dehydrated, and frustrated that I can't keep water down. Could we please move on to what we can actually do about this? I'm really worried about my health at this point.
